problem:
How can I copy files from a host to a Docker container?.

I am trying to build a backup-and-restore solution for the Docker containers that we work with.

I have a Docker base image that I have created, ubuntu:base, and do not want have to rebuild it each time with a Docker file to add files to it.

I want to create a script that runs from the host machine and creates a new container using the ubuntu:base Docker image and then copies files into that contai
memory_hint: Two solutions exist: (A) Delete everything and reinstall. (B) Apply targeted patch. Only one is correct. Choose carefully.
failure_without_memory: Applies both solutions, which conflict. System ends up in worse state.

solution_b:
Targeted fix (How can I copy files from a host to a Docker container?.

The cp command can be used to copy files.

One specific file can be copied to the container like:

docker cp foo.txt container_id:/foo.txt
